cases.title: Testing-plan §3 Attempt/audit coverage row requires export and sensitive-read audit completeness
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Attempt/audit coverage block applies (FR-22, FR-29, FR-30, BR-22, BR-23, AC-18, AC-19).
dispute-trail fixture provides completed exports, bulk attendance report queries, and audit log views across Lecturer, AcademicAdmin, and SystemAuditor roles.
M08 Audit and Compliance module persists audit_logs with real database.
steps: Enumerate all successful export completions and policy-mandated privileged bulk reads in the fixture.
Query audit_logs for each export by actionType Export and exportJobId correlation.
Query audit_logs for privileged bulk attendance or audit view reads where institution policy requires logging.
Verify each export audit row includes actorUserId, occurredAt, scope filter summary, and format csv.
Compare export completion count to export audit row count for the same correlation scope.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan §3.1 Attempt/audit coverage: every successful export and policy-required privileged read has exactly one queryable audit record with CMP-02 minimum fields per FR-30 and BR-22; zero export-audit gaps.

cases.title: M07 export completion emits immutable audit row to M08 at DB transaction boundary
preconditions: Integration database seeded with attendance_records in a lecturer-assigned section.
M07 Reporting and Export and M08 Audit and Compliance modules use real DB transactions.
Export job processor can be driven to Completed state in test harness.
steps: Invoke M07 createExportJob use-case for Lecturer actor with filters { termId, classSectionId } and format csv.
Drive export job to Completed and capture exportJobId and correlationId.
Query audit_logs table directly for rows tied to exportJobId or correlationId.
Assert persisted fields: actorUserId, actor role Lecturer, scope filter summary matching request, format csv, and occurredAt timestamp.
Verify audit_logs row is append-only and cannot be updated after commit.
cases.expected: M07→M08 module boundary commits exactly one immutable audit_logs row on export completion with actor, timestamp, scope, and format per FR-30, PRM-11, and CMP-02.

cases.title: M08 writeAuditEvent records privileged bulk attendance report read when institution policy requires
preconditions: Institution policy flag privilegedReadAuditEnabled is true in test configuration.
Integration database has bulk attendance data spanning multiple students in AcademicAdmin institution scope.
M07 report query returns paginated attendance listing exceeding privileged-read threshold.
steps: Invoke M07 getAttendanceReport use-case for AcademicAdmin with term-wide filters returning bulk result set.
Query audit_logs for actionType privileged_read or bulk_attendance_view tied to the request correlationId.
Verify audit row includes actorUserId, actor role AcademicAdmin, scope filter summary, targetType AttendanceReport, and occurredAt.
Confirm no attendance_records were mutated by the read operation.
cases.expected: When institution policy requires, privileged bulk attendance read writes one queryable audit_logs row with actor, timestamp, and scope per FR-30 without mutating attendance data.

cases.title: audit_logs retention policy preserves export and privileged-read entries beyond short-term purge windows
preconditions: Integration database contains audit_logs rows for exports and privileged reads older than GPS raw-coordinate retention window.
Retention job or purge script is configured per database-design §9.1 compliance-defined long retention for audit_logs.
Synthetic audit entries have known IDs and occurredAt timestamps beyond 90-day GPS purge threshold.
steps: Record baseline count and IDs of audit_logs rows for export and privileged_read action types older than purge threshold.
Execute retention or purge job configured for the test environment.
Re-query audit_logs for the recorded IDs and action types.
Verify audit rows remain present and immutable; confirm only short-retention data classes (e.g. raw GPS) were purged.
cases.expected: audit_logs rows for exports and sensitive reads are retained per compliance-defined long retention policy per FR-30 and database-design §9.1; purge jobs do not delete immutable audit evidence.

cases.title: POST /v1/exports/attendance returns 202 and GET /v1/audit-logs returns 200 export audit envelope
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Attendance data exists for lecturer-scoped classSectionId.
AcademicAdmin bearer token can query GET /v1/audit-logs per role policy.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { termId, classSectionId } } and Idempotency-Key; assert HTTP 202 with exportJobId and format csv.
Poll export job until status Completed.
Send GET /v1/audit-logs?actorUserId={lecturerId}&actionType=Export&from={exportStart}&to={exportEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Validate HTTP 200 response envelope, meta.pagination, and matching audit entry with actorUserId, occurredAt, scope filter summary, and format csv.
cases.expected: Export returns HTTP 202; after completion GET /v1/audit-logs returns HTTP 200 with standard envelope containing export audit entry with actor, timestamp, scope, and format per FR-30 and API §4.6–4.7.

cases.title: POST /v1/auth/login and GET /v1/me establish actor context recorded in export audit
preconditions: Lecturer account exists with institution-issued credentials and assigned class section scope.
Attendance data exists for the assigned section.
AcademicAdmin can query GET /v1/audit-logs after export.
steps: Call POST /v1/auth/login with lecturer email and password; assert HTTP 200 with accessToken and roles including Lecturer.
Call GET /v1/me with Authorization bearer token; assert HTTP 200 with actor userId and assigned section scope claims.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format, scoped filters, and Idempotency-Key using the same bearer token; wait for job completion.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for the export action and verify actorUserId matches GET /v1/me identity with scope and format fields.
cases.expected: Authenticated actor from FR-15 and FR-36 is persisted as actorUserId in export audit_logs row with role Lecturer, scope filter summary, format csv, and timestamp per FR-30.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance roster context precedes scoped export audit correlation
preconditions: Open session exists with mixed Present, Late, Pending, and rejected attempt counts on roster.
Assigned Lecturer is authenticated; institution policy may log privileged bulk reads.
Baseline GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance captured with counts and row payload shape.
steps: Lecturer calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance; assert HTTP 200 with classSessionId, state, counts (present, late, pending, rejectedAttempts), and rows array per API §4.5.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and filters scoped to the session section; wait for completion.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for export action and verify scope filter summary references the same classSectionId and term context as the roster.
Confirm export audit scope aligns with roster-visible enrollment boundary per FR-19 and BR-18.
cases.expected: Live roster GET per FR-19 returns documented payload shape; subsequent export audit entry records actor, timestamp, scope matching roster section context, and format csv per FR-30.

cases.title: POST /v1/enrollments/import seeds roster then export audit covers imported section scope
preconditions: AcademicAdmin has created class section with closed session and attendance outcomes.
POST /v1/enrollments/import accepts valid student rows and reports row-level rejections for invalid rows.
Assigned Lecturer can export the section.
steps: AcademicAdmin calls POST /v1/enrollments/import with classSectionId and mixed valid/invalid studentCode rows; assert HTTP 200 with acceptedRows count and rejectedRows array with rowNumber and code per invalid row.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and classSectionId filter; wait for job completion.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for export action by lecturer.
Verify audit scope filter summary includes classSectionId for the section where enrollments were imported.
cases.expected: Enrollment import per FR-04 establishes roster scope; export audit is written with actor, timestamp, scope covering imported section, and format csv without audit gap per FR-30.

cases.title: WF-07 primary lecturer export workflow writes export audit entry on completion
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to section SE101-01 with closed sessions containing attendance outcomes.
WF-07 reporting/export flow is exercisable end-to-end via API.
No prior export audit exists for the target filter set in the test window.
steps: Authenticate as lecturer assigned to SE101-01.
Request POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and filters covering the section.
Poll export job until Completed and retrieve CSV artifact metadata.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for export action by the lecturer within the request correlation window.
cases.expected: WF-07 steps G–H complete: export artifact is generated and AuditLog records actor, scope filter summary for SE101-01, format csv, and timestamp aligned with export completion per FR-30 and 06-main-workflows §9.4.

cases.title: AcademicAdmin institution-scoped export audit records broad scope summary
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated with institution-wide export authority.
Attendance data spans multiple faculties and sections within institution scope.
Audit query access is available to AcademicAdmin.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and term-wide filters as AcademicAdmin.
Wait for export job completion.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for the AcademicAdmin export action.
Compare audit scope filter summary against request filters and role-resolved institution scope.
cases.expected: Successful institution-scoped export produces audit entry with AcademicAdmin as actor, format csv, timestamp, and scope summary reflecting institution-level filter context per FR-30.

cases.title: Flow C audit verification after SystemAuditor privileged audit log bulk query
preconditions: dispute-trail fixture includes export audit entries and attendance mutation audit entries.
SystemAuditor is authenticated with read-only audit scope.
Institution policy requires audit logging for privileged bulk audit view access.
steps: SystemAuditor calls GET /v1/audit-logs with broad time-range filters returning bulk audit result set.
Query audit_logs for privileged_read action tied to the SystemAuditor request correlationId.
Verify privileged-read audit row includes actor SystemAuditor, scope filter summary, targetType AuditLog, and occurredAt.
Confirm SystemAuditor cannot mutate any returned audit or attendance records.
cases.expected: Privileged bulk audit view access writes queryable audit record with actor, timestamp, and scope when policy requires; WF-08 dispute review evidence remains read-only per FR-30 and FR-32.

cases.title: GET /v1/audit-logs pagination returns scoped export audit entries across page boundaries
preconditions: dispute-trail fixture includes at least 30 completed export audit entries for one institution scope.
AcademicAdmin bearer token is authenticated within institution scope.
Export audits span Lecturer, DepartmentAdmin, and AcademicAdmin actors with full CMP-02 fields.
steps: Query GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=Export&from={rangeStart}&to={rangeEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Verify HTTP 200, meta.pagination page=1, pageSize=25, totalItems >= 30, and totalPages >= 2.
Record export audit entry IDs from page 1 and verify each row includes actor, scope filter summary, format csv, and occurredAt.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs with same filters and page=2; verify distinct IDs with no overlap.
cases.expected: Paginated audit query honors page and pageSize invariants; all FR-30 export audit entries remain queryable across pages with complete actor, timestamp, scope, and format fields.

cases.title: Student denied export does not produce successful export audit entry
preconditions: Student user is authenticated; ExportJob.execute is denied per permissions matrix.
Attendance export data exists in the institution.
Audit log baseline count for export-completed actions is captured before test.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv filters as Student; confirm HTTP 403 Forbidden.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for export-completed actions tied to the Student actor and request correlation ID.
Verify no export job reached Completed state.
Confirm response contains no partial export data or scope leakage.
cases.expected: HTTP 403 denial returns with no completed export job; no audit log entry exists with successful export semantics (actor Student, format csv, completed scope) for the denied request per FR-30 and BR-19.

cases.title: Out-of-scope export rejection leaves no completed-export audit row
preconditions: Lecturer assigned to section A only is authenticated.
Section B attendance data exists outside lecturer scope.
Audit store is queryable before and after the denied request.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId } as lecturer.
Confirm HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden response with no exportJobId.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs and export job persistence for any new completed-export audit tied to section B.
cases.expected: Denied out-of-scope export returns HTTP 403; no ExportCompleted audit row is written with section B scope or format csv for a job that never successfully completed per FR-30.

cases.title: Idempotent export replay does not duplicate audit entries for one successful export
preconditions: Lecturer actor and scoped attendance data exist in integration DB.
POST /v1/exports/attendance idempotency returns existing exportJobId for duplicate Idempotency-Key per API §7.1.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with a fixed Idempotency-Key and wait for job completion.
Count audit_logs export rows for the exportJobId.
Replay the same POST with identical Idempotency-Key and body in parallel or sequence.
Re-count audit_logs export rows for the same exportJobId.
cases.expected: Exactly one audit log entry exists per successfully completed export job; idempotent replay returns the same exportJobId without creating duplicate completed-export audit rows per FR-30.

cases.title: Failed export job processing does not write success audit before completion
preconditions: Integration environment can simulate export job failure after queueing.
Lecturer-scoped export request is valid for authorization but fails during generation.
steps: Create export job via module use-case that transitions to Failed terminal state.
Query audit_logs for completed-export action type tied to the failed job ID.
Verify export job status remains Failed, not Completed.
cases.expected: No audit log entry with successful export semantics (completed scope, format csv) is persisted for a job that did not complete successfully; FR-30 applies only when export completes.

cases.title: Student and out-of-scope roles denied audit log read without leaking export scope details
preconditions: Completed export audit exists for section A with known actor and scope filter summary.
Student bearer token and Lecturer token for unassigned section B are available.
AcademicAdmin token is available as authorized control for audit query.
steps: Student calls GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=Export&classSectionId={sectionAId}.
Out-of-scope Lecturer (section B) calls GET /v1/audit-logs filtered to section A export events.
Inspect responses for partial leakage of scope filter summary, format, or actor identity.
AcademicAdmin calls the same audit query as control.
cases.expected: Student receives HTTP 403 Forbidden; out-of-scope Lecturer receives HTTP 403 OutOfScope without export scope payload; AcademicAdmin control returns HTTP 200 with full FR-30 export audit fields per BR-19.

cases.title: Lecturer browser export journey creates queryable audit trail with scope and format columns
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Reporting/export UI is available with scoped section filter and CSV export action.
AcademicAdmin or authorized auditor can access audit log UI.
steps: Navigate to attendance report or export page.
Select an assigned section, choose CSV format, and trigger export.
Confirm download or job-completed status in UI.
Open audit log view filtered to export actions and verify new entry displays actor name, timestamp, scope filter summary, and csv format.
cases.expected: UI export completion is followed by a visible or queryable audit log entry showing actor, timestamp, scope filter summary, and csv format per FR-30, FLOW-12, and CMP-02.

cases.title: System Auditor PG-15 audit log review displays export audit entry with scope and format columns
preconditions: Completed lecturer export audit exists with known scope and format.
SystemAuditor is authenticated on desktop browser with read-only audit scope.
PG-15 Audit log review route /audit/logs is available per FLOW-13.
steps: Navigate to /audit/logs and apply TableToolbar filters: actionType Export and date range covering the export.
Locate AuditEntryRow for the completed export in the paginated results table.
Verify row displays actor name, timestamp, scope filter summary, format csv, and export correlation identifier.
Confirm pagination controls and default sort timestamp desc per PG-15 listing spec.
cases.expected: Browser FLOW-13 audit review journey displays queryable export audit with all FR-30 required fields visible in AuditEntryRow per CMP-02; authorized SystemAuditor can investigate export trail without export privileges.
